name: Limantour Beach
access: Public NPS beach reached from the Limantour parking area; verify wildlife closures and surf conditions before leaving.
depthProfile: Broad shallow sand flat that deepens gradually outside the first breakers.
parking: Free NPS lot; fills on summer weekends.
transit: No regular transit; long bicycle approach from Bear Valley.

name: Drakes Beach
access: Public NPS beach below the Kenneth C. Patrick Visitor Center; seasonal wildlife restrictions can affect nearby shoreline.
depthProfile: Protected sandy shelf with a gentler nearshore slope than the outer Point Reyes beaches.
parking: Large free NPS lot.
transit: No regular public transit.

name: Point Reyes South Beach
access: Public NPS ocean beach from the South Beach lot; exposed surf, sneaker waves, and seasonal closures make a same-day access check essential.
depthProfile: Steep high-energy beach face with mobile bars and troughs.
parking: Free NPS lot with a short sand walk.
transit: No regular public transit.

name: Bolinas Beach
access: Public sandy beach reached at the end of Brighton Avenue; stay clear of the Bolinas Lagoon harbor entrance and private property.
depthProfile: Shallow sandy embayment beside a tidal-lagoon channel.
parking: Very limited street parking; do not block driveways.
transit: Marin Transit serves downtown Bolinas on limited schedules.

name: Stinson Beach
access: Public GGNRA beach; fish outside guarded swim zones and confirm posted restrictions.
depthProfile: Long sandy beach with seasonal troughs and bars.
parking: Large free lot that fills early on warm days.
transit: Marin Transit route 61 serves Stinson Beach.

name: Muir Beach
access: Public GGNRA cove reached by a footbridge; small beach and strong shorebreak require extra spacing and caution.
depthProfile: Compact sand-and-gravel cove bounded by rocky points.
parking: Free GGNRA lot; capacity is limited.
transit: No regular public transit.

name: Rodeo Beach
access: Public GGNRA beach at Fort Cronkhite; exposed shorebreak and fast-changing surf demand conservative judgment.
depthProfile: Coarse sand and gravel beach with a steep face near a lagoon outlet.
parking: Free Fort Cronkhite lots.
transit: Seasonal Muni 76X service varies; verify schedule.

name: McNears Beach Pier
access: Public Marin County fishing pier inside McNears Beach Park; park hours and vehicle entry fees apply.
depthProfile: Shallow bay margin dropping toward the San Rafael channel system.
parking: Paid park entry with parking near the pier.
transit: No direct regular transit.

name: Paradise Beach Pier
access: Public Marin County fishing pier in Paradise Beach Park; posted park hours apply. Marin County eliminated park entry fees in 2023.
depthProfile: Pier reaches from a narrow rocky-sand shelf toward deeper central-bay water.
parking: Free park entry and an on-site lot close to the pier; capacity can be limited at busy times.
transit: No direct regular transit.
accessStatusNote: Open public park and fishing pier during posted hours; park entry fees were eliminated in 2023.

name: Fort Baker Fishing Pier
access: Public GGNRA fishing pier at Horseshoe Cove; strong Golden Gate current can make some tide stages unfishable.
depthProfile: Protected cove shelf close to a steep, high-current channel edge.
parking: Free and paid spaces around Fort Baker; obey posted restrictions.
transit: Golden Gate Transit stops require a walk; bicycle access available.

name: Torpedo Wharf
access: Public NPS fishing pier east of Fort Point; cracked and heaving asphalt creates uneven surfaces, and the wharf is exposed to strong current, wind, and large vessel wakes.
depthProfile: Narrow shelf at the edge of the Golden Gate channel.
parking: Limited free and time-restricted parking near Fort Point; spaces can fill quickly.
transit: Muni routes and the Presidio shuttle stop within walking distance.
accessStatusNote: The wharf is public, but NPS notes cracked and heaving asphalt; use care on uneven surfaces.

name: Crissy Field East Beach
access: Public GGNRA beach; share the shoreline with swimmers, boardsports users, and dogs, and avoid signed restoration areas.
depthProfile: Shallow sand flat with a gradual slope toward the Golden Gate approach channel.
parking: Paid lots and limited free spaces around Crissy Field.
transit: Muni and Presidio shuttle stops nearby.

name: Baker Beach
access: Public GGNRA beach; powerful shorebreak and rip currents are common, and the north end is clothing optional.
depthProfile: Steep sandy beach beside rocky Golden Gate headlands.
parking: Free NPS lots with limited capacity.
transit: Muni route 29 stops near the access road.

name: China Beach
access: Public GGNRA cove reached by stairs and a steep path; confirm construction or access notices before visiting.
depthProfile: Small sandy pocket framed by rocky points and a nearshore slope break.
parking: Very limited street parking.
transit: Muni routes stop in Sea Cliff within walking distance.

name: Ocean Beach North
access: Public GGNRA beach near Balboa Street; high-energy surf, sneaker waves, and shifting bars require conservative conditions.
depthProfile: High-energy sand beach with mobile bars and deep troughs.
parking: Large roadside lots and street parking.
transit: Muni lines 5R, 31, and N Judah serve nearby access points.

name: Ocean Beach South
access: Public GGNRA beach south of Sloat Boulevard; check temporary closures and erosion work, and avoid heavy surf.
depthProfile: Broad sand beach with shifting nearshore bars and cuts.
parking: Roadside parking varies with construction and closures.
transit: Muni L bus and route 23 serve the Sloat area.

name: Pier 7
access: Public Port of San Francisco pier; keep clear of promenade users and obey posted fishing restrictions.
depthProfile: Urban pier over a sloping mud-and-sand shelf close to deeper ferry water.
parking: Paid garages and meters; costly at peak times.
transit: Muni F line, buses, BART, and ferries are nearby.

name: Pier 14
access: Public Port of San Francisco pedestrian pier; fish only where posted rules allow and yield to other pier users.
depthProfile: Pier reaches across a sloping urban shelf into faster central-bay water.
parking: Paid garages and meters along the Embarcadero.
transit: Muni, BART, and ferries are within walking distance.

name: Crane Cove Park
access: Public Port of San Francisco shoreline park; use designated public edges and avoid swim and launch areas.
depthProfile: Protected cove with a shallow sand-mud shelf near old maritime structures.
parking: Metered street parking; limited free spaces farther inland.
transit: Muni T line and buses stop nearby.

name: Heron's Head Park Pier
access: Public fishing pier and shoreline at Heron's Head Park; protect restored wetlands and respect any posted habitat closures.
depthProfile: Very shallow mud-sand flats cut by a modest tidal channel.
parking: Free lot near the park entrance.
transit: Muni T line and route 19 require a moderate walk.

name: Ferry Point Fishing Pier
access: Public EBRPD pier in Miller/Knox Regional Shoreline; check park hours and any pier maintenance notices.
depthProfile: Pier crosses a shallow sand-mud shelf toward a San Pablo Bay channel edge.
parking: Free park lot near Ferry Point.
transit: AC Transit service to Point Richmond followed by a walk or bicycle ride.

name: Keller Beach
access: Public EBRPD beach in Miller/Knox; fish away from swimmers and confirm water-contact advisories.
depthProfile: Protected sandy pocket with rocky edges and a gradual bayward slope.
parking: Free park lots; a short walk to the beach.
transit: AC Transit to Point Richmond, then walk or bicycle.

name: Point Isabel Shoreline
access: Public EBRPD shoreline; the area is heavily used by off-leash dogs, so keep hooks and bait controlled and fish only from durable public edges.
depthProfile: Very shallow mud-sand flat with small tidal drains.
parking: Free lots, often busy.
transit: AC Transit 72-series buses and Bay Trail bicycle access.

name: Albany Bulb Shoreline
access: Public waterfront reached on rough trails; fish from stable shoreline only and avoid unstable rubble and restoration areas.
depthProfile: Riprap peninsula surrounded by shallow flats with a sharper western edge.
parking: Free lot at the Albany waterfront.
transit: AC Transit and Bay Trail access.

name: Berkeley Marina North Basin Shore
access: Public marina shoreline and rock wall; Berkeley Pier is closed, so this location deliberately excludes the closed pier deck.
depthProfile: Riprap edge bordering a dredged marina basin and shallow exterior flat.
parking: Free and metered marina parking depending on area.
transit: AC Transit route 51B and Bay Trail bicycle access.

name: Cesar Chavez Park Shoreline
access: Public Berkeley shoreline; use stable riprap access points and keep clear of kite, dog, and trail users.
depthProfile: Riprap peninsula with shallow flats and a modest outer slope break.
parking: Free lots and street parking at the marina.
transit: AC Transit and Bay Trail bicycle access.

name: Emeryville Marina Fishing Pier
access: Public fishing pier at Emeryville Marina; verify posted hours and temporary maintenance closures.
depthProfile: Pier reaches from riprap across a shallow shelf near a dredged marina approach.
parking: Public marina lots; obey permit and time-limit signs.
transit: Emery Go-Round and Bay Trail bicycle access.

name: Port View Park Fishing Pier
access: Public fishing pier at the west end of 7th Street; observe Port of Oakland security boundaries and posted park hours.
depthProfile: Pier over the dredged Oakland Outer Harbor margin.
parking: Free lot when the park is open.
transit: AC Transit route 72M stops on 7th Street; long bicycle access from BART.

name: Middle Harbor Shoreline Park
access: Public shoreline park with designated fishing areas; stay outside habitat restoration zones and Port security boundaries.
depthProfile: Restored shallow cove beside the dredged Oakland harbor channel.
parking: Free park lot during posted hours.
transit: Limited AC Transit; bicycle access from West Oakland BART.

name: Alameda South Shore Rock Wall
access: Public bayfront segments along Shore Line Drive; use legal public access points and avoid protected bird areas and private marina property.
depthProfile: Riprap edge fronting an extensive shallow sand flat.
parking: Street and beach parking; restrictions vary by segment.
transit: AC Transit buses and Cross Alameda Trail connections.

name: Crown Memorial State Beach
access: Public state beach managed by EBRPD; fish outside swim zones and heed seasonal wildlife and water-quality notices.
depthProfile: Very broad, shallow sand flat with small tidal channels.
parking: Paid park lot plus nearby street parking.
transit: AC Transit buses and bicycle routes serve the shoreline.

name: Oyster Bay Regional Shoreline
access: Public EBRPD shoreline; fish from stable public edges and stay out of marsh restoration areas.
depthProfile: Shallow east-bay mud flat beside remediated peninsula riprap.
parking: Free park lots during posted hours.
transit: AC Transit and Bay Trail bicycle access.

name: San Leandro Marina Shore
access: Public shoreline around Marina Park; redevelopment can change access, so follow construction signs and use designated edges.
depthProfile: Riprap and marina mouth adjoining broad shallow flats.
parking: Free city park lots where open.
transit: AC Transit connections and Bay Trail access.

name: Dumbarton Fishing Pier
access: Public fishing pier at Don Edwards San Francisco Bay National Wildlife Refuge; refuge hours and wildlife protections apply.
depthProfile: Long pier crosses expansive mud flats toward the former rail alignment and deeper tidal flow.
parking: Free refuge lot; gate hours apply.
transit: No convenient regular transit; Bay Trail bicycle approach available.

name: Coyote Point Jetty
access: Public San Mateo County park shoreline and jetty; park entry fee and hours apply, and rocks can be slick.
depthProfile: Rocky point and marina entrance beside a shallow sand-mud shelf.
parking: Paid county park entry with multiple lots.
transit: Caltrain Burlingame or SamTrans plus a long walk/bicycle ride.

name: Seal Point Park Shoreline
access: Public San Mateo shoreline; fish only from stable public riprap and keep hooks controlled around dogs and Bay Trail users.
depthProfile: Shallow sand-mud flats with riprap and small tidal drains.
parking: Free city lot.
transit: SamTrans and Bay Trail bicycle access.

name: Oyster Point Fishing Pier
access: Public pier near Oyster Point Marina; redevelopment and maintenance can change routes, so follow posted access signs.
depthProfile: Pier and rock edge adjacent to a dredged marina basin and channel shoulder.
parking: Public marina and park lots; restrictions vary.
transit: SamTrans, Caltrain shuttle connections, and Bay Trail access.

name: Pacifica Municipal Pier
access: Closed to the public since June 4, 2026, while the City of Pacifica assesses and repairs storm-related structural damage. Do not enter the pier; check the City's official status page before travel.
depthProfile: Long pier over a sandy nearshore shelf with seasonal bars.
parking: Street and public lots near Sharp Park; restrictions apply.
transit: SamTrans routes 110 and 112 serve nearby stops.
accessStatusNote: Closed beginning June 4, 2026, because storm damage compromised structural members. Reopening timing has not been announced.

name: Sharp Park Beach
access: Public city beach south of the pier; exposed surf and a steep beach face require careful conditions.
depthProfile: Dark sand beach with a steep inner trough and seasonal bars.
parking: Street and public lots around Sharp Park.
transit: SamTrans routes serve Palmetto Avenue.

name: Rockaway Beach
access: Public city beach in a small cove; fish only in manageable surf and give swimmers and surfers room.
depthProfile: Compact sand cove bounded by rocky headlands and a defined trough.
parking: Public lots and street spaces near Rockaway Beach Avenue.
transit: SamTrans route 110 serves Rockaway Beach.

name: Pacifica State Beach (Linda Mar)
access: Public city beach with heavy surfing use; fish at uncrowded edges and respect protected habitat near San Pedro Creek.
depthProfile: Curving sand beach with a creek mouth, bars, and a deeper southern pocket.
parking: Paid city lots and limited street parking.
transit: SamTrans routes 110 and 112 serve Linda Mar.

name: Montara State Beach
access: Public state beach; steep paths, strong shorebreak, and rip currents require conservative access and surf choices.
depthProfile: Steep sand beach bracketed by rocky reef and headlands.
parking: Free roadside lots on Highway 1; use marked crossings.
transit: SamTrans route 117 has limited coastal service.

name: Pillar Point Harbor West Jetty
access: Public harbor jetty approached from the Mavericks area; remain on stable surfaces and never climb wave-washed outer rocks.
depthProfile: Rock jetty separating a protected harbor basin from a deeper ocean channel.
parking: Public harbor and Mavericks lots; some spaces are restricted.
transit: SamTrans route 117 serves Princeton with a walk to the jetty.

name: Pillar Point Harbor East Jetty
access: Public harbor edge reached near Princeton; use only stable, dry access and respect posted harbor operations and closures.
depthProfile: Rock jetty beside a dredged harbor entrance and shallow sand beach.
parking: Public harbor lots with posted restrictions.
transit: SamTrans route 117 serves Princeton.

name: Surfer's Beach
access: Public beach south of the harbor; heavy surfing use and ongoing erosion mean access points can change.
depthProfile: Sand beach beside the harbor jetty, with a pronounced trough and structure transition.
parking: Highway 1 pullouts and harbor parking; obey signs.
transit: SamTrans route 117 serves Princeton and El Granada.

name: Francis State Beach
access: Public state beach in Half Moon Bay State Beach; fish away from swimmers and check campground and day-use hours.
depthProfile: Long sand beach with mobile bars, cuts, and a moderate nearshore slope.
parking: Paid state park day-use lot.
transit: SamTrans route 294 plus a walk; Coastal Trail bicycle access.

name: Poplar Beach
access: Public city beach reached by a steep bluff trail; high tides can reduce usable beach and dogs and horses are common.
depthProfile: Broad sand beach with seasonal bars and a steeper outer trough.
parking: Paid city lot at the west end of Poplar Street.
transit: Limited local transit; Coastal Trail bicycle access.
